message:
fix(runtime): cancel-aware waker routing, event drop fix, and copy yield budgets

Three correctness fixes in the runtime layer:

- ThreeLaneWaker and ThreeLaneLocalWaker now inspect cx_inner.cancel_requested
  and route cancelled tasks to the cancel lane instead of the ready lane,
  preserving the "losers are drained" invariant during I/O wakeups
- Events::push() no longer silently drops events beyond capacity; dropping
  edge-triggered notifications is a correctness bug, so the vector is
  allowed to grow unbounded
- Epoll reactor uses hysteresis (4x threshold) when shrinking the
  poll_events buffer to avoid reallocation thrash after burst activity
- Copy, CopyBuf, CopyWithProgress gain a yield_counter (budget of 32)
  and CopyBidirectional gains a YIELD_BUDGET of 64 steps to prevent
  scheduler starvation on fast streams; also fixes a logic inversion in
  CopyBidirectional's progress-tracking step counter

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
